---
title: "Work with me"
subtitle: "Selective consulting on credible impact evaluation & measurement."
---

I take on a small number of projects where the question is "did this actually
work, and how do we know?" — the kind of thing this whole site is about.

## What I help with

- **Impact evaluation design & analysis** — matching the question to a defensible
  design (RD, DiD, matching, RCTs), and the analysis that follows
- **Baseline equivalence & WWC-aligned reporting** — the tables and effect sizes
  reviewers and funders expect (this is what [`baselinr`](https://github.com/zl1212-ship-it/baselinr)
  automates)
- **Measurement & instrument validation** — reliability, validity, measurement
  invariance before you compare groups
- **Computational text analysis** — turning open-ended responses, feedback, or
  policy text into analysis-ready data, without pretending the classifier is
  ground truth

## Who I work with

Education researchers, school districts and systems, ed-tech teams, nonprofits,
and foundations that need an effectiveness claim to hold up — whether that's
designing an evaluation, reviewing one, or rescuing an analysis that's gone
sideways.

## Get in touch

Tell me a bit about the project and what decision it's informing, and I'll tell
you honestly whether and how I can help.

[**→ Email me**](mailto:zl1212@uw.edu?subject=Consulting%20inquiry&body=Hi%20Yuxia%2C%20here%27s%20the%20project%20I%27m%20thinking%20about%3A)

Background: Yuxia Liang, M.Ed. in Educational Leadership & Policy, University of
Washington; author of the open-source `baselinr` R package.
